title: 1.1: Tests that bidirectional streaming creates a stream connection
1.2: Tests that bidirectional streaming sends requests through the stream
1.3: Tests that bidirectional streaming receives and validates responses
1.4: Tests that stream cancellation creates a stream with context.WithCancel
1.5: Tests that stream cancellation calls the cancel function
1.6: Tests that stream cancellation verifies server receives cancellation
1.7: Tests that stream cancellation checks for context.Canceled error
1.8: Tests that deadline propagation creates a stream with context.WithTimeout or context.WithDeadline
1.9: Tests that deadline propagation waits for deadline to be exceeded
1.10: Tests that deadline propagation checks for context.DeadlineExceeded error
1.11: Tests that output congestion has the server send multiple responses
1.12: Tests that output congestion verifies other operations complete without blocking
1.13: Tests that input congestion has the client send multiple requests
1.14: Tests that input congestion verifies other operations complete without blocking
2.1: Places the bidirectional streaming test in internal/grid directory
2.2: Places the stream cancellation test in internal/grid directory
2.3: Places the deadline propagation test in internal/grid directory
2.4: Places the output congestion test in internal/grid directory
2.5: Places the input congestion test in internal/grid directory
3.1: All tests use the Go testing package with *testing.T parameter
3.2: All tests use the grid Manager type for node setup
3.3: All streaming tests use the grid package streaming APIs for creating streams
3.4: All tests use t.Fatal, t.Error, or t.Errorf for assertions
4.1: Follows grid package handler registration pattern for streaming tests
4.2: Follows t.Run subtest pattern with descriptive names for tests with subtests
